title: 15 MCP tools — well beyond the spec's query set
evidence: lib/br_soccer/mcp/tools.ex:159-353 defines search_matches, head_to_head, last_match, team_record, team_competitions, league_standings, relegated_teams, search_players, player_profile, brazilian_clubs_squads, competition_stats, biggest_wins, top_scoring_teams, team_rankings, compare_seasons

title: Hand-rolled CSV parser and MCP transport with no third-party protocol libs
evidence: lib/br_soccer/csv.ex (RFC 4180 parser) and lib/br_soccer/mcp/server.ex (JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio); only dependency is jason (mix.exs:24)
suggestion: None — reduces dependency surface; verified by csv_test.exs and mcp_test.exs.

title: Cross-source fixture de-duplication by source priority
evidence: lib/br_soccer/loader.ex:1282-1296 dedups {competition,season,home_key,away_key}; lib/br_soccer/competitions.ex:1042-1088 picks one source per season for standings
suggestion: None — avoids double-counting across overlapping CSVs (R5/R9).

title: token_efficiency scored 0.0
suggestion: Cost/efficiency signal only — the run produced a large, thorough codebase (~2273 lib LOC). Not a correctness defect; noted for cross-run comparison.

title: README states 57 tests; source defines 58 test macros
evidence: README.md '57 tests cover...'; grep counts 58 `test ` macros across test/*.exs
suggestion: Cosmetic count drift; both indicate a full passing suite (test_coverage=1.0). Update README if precision matters.
